---
title: "Packages"
description: "The EpiAware ecosystem — small, composable packages that combine into infectious disease models."
listing:
  id: package-listing
  contents:
    - "*.qmd"
  type: grid
  grid-columns: 2
  fields: [title, description, categories, status]
  field-display-names:
    status: "Status"
  sort: false
  filter-ui: false
  categories: true
---

Each package does one thing and combines with the others. Most extend Julia's
[Distributions.jl](https://github.com/JuliaStats/Distributions.jl) so they share
a common interface and compose cleanly. Read any package's documentation in the
[unified docs browser](../docs.qmd), or follow the links on each page below.

Each card carries a maturity **status**, based on where the package sits in its
release cycle rather than a subjective judgement:

- [**experimental**]{.status-pill .status-experimental} — usable, but not yet a
  registered release; install from GitHub and expect the API to change.
- [**maturing**]{.status-pill .status-maturing} — registered and installable with
  `Pkg.add`, on a `0.x` line while the API settles.
- [**stable**]{.status-pill .status-stable} — a `1.0`+ release with a committed,
  semantically versioned API. *(none yet)*

::: {#package-listing}
:::

```{=html}
<script>
// Render each card's plain "Status" field as a coloured pill matching the
// legend. Degrades gracefully to the default label/value table if JS is off.
document.querySelectorAll('.quarto-grid-item table.card-other-values').forEach(function (table) {
  var cell = table.querySelector('td.status');
  if (!cell) return;
  var value = cell.textContent.trim().toLowerCase();
  if (!value) return;
  var wrap = document.createElement('div');
  wrap.className = 'card-status';
  wrap.innerHTML = '<span class="status-pill status-' + value + '"><strong>' + value + '</strong></span>';
  table.replaceWith(wrap);
});
</script>
```
